text: Designing a good REST API is essential for creating user-friendly web services. Start by keeping your endpoints simple and logical. For example, use /users for a list of users and /users/123 for a specific user. Always use appropriate HTTP status codes, like 200 for success, 201 for created, 404 for not found, and 500 for server errors. Versioning is also critical; include a version number in your URL, such as /v1/users, so changes don't break existing clients. Finally, provide clear documentation explaining how to use each endpoint, including request examples and error messages. Good documentation saves time for developers and helps them integrate smoothly with your API.
